---
title: "Fig 1: Sampling Map"
output: html_document
date: "2025-01-03"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Load libraries 
```{r libraries}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggspatial)
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)
library(sf)
library(rnaturalearth) # install.packages("terra", type = "binary")
library(rnaturalearthdata)
```

# Create and Plot Maps
```{r North America Map}
north_america <- ne_countries(continent = "North America", returnclass = "sf")

# Plot the filtered map with a square added
na_map <- 
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = north_america, fill = "lightgrey", color = "black") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-105, -75), ylim = c(15, 35)) +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = -84, xmax = -80, ymin = 24, ymax = 26), 
            fill = "transparent", size = 0.75, alpha = 0.5, color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-105, -75, by = 15)) +  
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(15, 35, by = 10)) +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
        panel.background = element_blank(), 
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 5)) +
  #annotation_scale(location = "br", width_hint = 0.16) +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  )

ggsave("Plots/Fig_1/na_map_plot.png", plot = na_map, width = 4, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r Florida Keys Map}
# Get spatial data for the USA
usa <- ne_states(country = "United States of America", returnclass = "sf")

# Filter to focus on Florida
florida <- usa[usa$name == "Florida", ]

# Define the bounding box for the Florida Keys
keys_bbox <- st_bbox(c(xmin = -82, xmax = -80, ymin = 24.5, ymax = 25.5), crs = st_crs(florida))

# Crop Florida data to the bounding box
florida_keys <- st_crop(florida, keys_bbox)

# Define sampling site coordinates (longitude, latitude)
sampling_sites <- data.frame(
  Site = c("Offshore", "Inshore"),
  Longitude = c(-81.48355, -81.42903),  # Converted W degrees to negative longitude
  Latitude = c(24.53145, 24.60577)     # Converted N degrees to decimal format
)

temperature_sites <- data.frame(
  Site = c("Offshore", "Inshore"),
  Longitude = c(-81.404000, -81.384000),  # Converted W degrees to negative longitude
  Latitude = c(24.546000, 24.615000)     # Converted N degrees to decimal format
)

# Convert to spatial object
sampling_sites_sf <- st_as_sf(sampling_sites, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326)

temperature_sites_sf <- st_as_sf(temperature_sites, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Add a new column 'Site_Type' to distinguish between Sampling and Temperature sites
sampling_sites_sf$Site_Type <- "Sampling"
temperature_sites_sf$Site_Type <- "Temperature"


# Florida Keys Zoomed In 
map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = florida, fill = "lightgrey", color = "black") + 
  geom_sf(data = florida_keys, fill = "lightgrey", color = "black") + 
  geom_sf(data = sampling_sites_sf, aes(color = Site, shape = Site_Type), size = 3.5) + 
  geom_point(data = temperature_sites_sf, aes(x = st_coordinates(geometry)[,1], y = st_coordinates(geometry)[,2], 
                                              color = Site, shape = Site_Type), size = 3.5) +  
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-81.8, -80.3), ylim = c(24.5, 25.4)) +  
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-82, -80, by = 0.5)) +  
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(24.4, 25.4, by = 0.2)) +  
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.background = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8), 
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(-0.35, "cm"),  
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm"),
    legend.position = c(0.86,0.17),  
    #legend.box = "horizontal",  # Arrange legends horizontally
    #legend.box.just = "center",  # Center the legends
    legend.background = element_blank()
  ) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Offshore" = "darkblue", "Inshore" = "lightblue")) + 
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(order = 1, ncol = 1),  # Sampling legend
    shape = guide_legend(order = 2, ncol = 1)  # Temperature legend
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Sampling" = 16, "Temperature" = 17), labels = c("Colony Collection", "Temperature Monitoring")) +
  annotation_scale(location = "br", width_hint = 0.2) +
  labs(
    x = "Longitude",
    y = "Latitude"
  )

# Save the map plot
ggsave("Plots/Fig_1/map_plot.png", plot = map, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

```{r Florida Keys Temperature Data}
#Newfound Harbor Spotter Buoy
inshore_temp <- read.csv("data/Environmental_Data/Inshore_data_site_3650_2024_10_19_2025_08_27.csv") %>%
  dplyr::select(timestamp, dhw_noaa, satellite_temperature_noaa, sst_anomaly_noaa,  bottom_temperature_spotter, top_temperature_spotter) %>%
  mutate(site = "Inshore")

# Looe Key Reef Buoy 
offshore_temp <- read.csv("data/Environmental_Data/Offshore_data_site_3649_2024_10_19_2025_08_28.csv") %>%
  dplyr::select(timestamp, dhw_noaa, satellite_temperature_noaa, sst_anomaly_noaa,  bottom_temperature_spotter, top_temperature_spotter) %>%
  mutate(site = "Offshore")

site_temps <- rbind(inshore_temp, offshore_temp) 

# Ensure the timestamp column is in datetime format
site_temps$timestamp <- as.POSIXct(site_temps$timestamp, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%OSZ", tz = "UTC")

# Filter the data for dates after 2024-10-22
site_temps_plot <- site_temps %>%
    filter(
    site == "Inshore" |
    (site == "Offshore" & timestamp <= as.POSIXct("2025-03-08", tz = "UTC")) |
    (site == "Offshore" & timestamp >= as.POSIXct("2025-05-06", tz = "UTC"))
  ) %>%
  mutate(segment = case_when(
    site == "Inshore"  ~ 1L,
    site == "Offshore" & timestamp <= as.POSIXct("2025-03-08", tz = "UTC") ~ 1L,
    site == "Offshore" & timestamp >= as.POSIXct("2025-05-06", tz = "UTC") ~ 2L
  ))


 temps<-
   ggplot(site_temps_plot, aes(x = timestamp, 
                       y = top_temperature_spotter, 
                       color = site, group = interaction(site, segment))) +
  geom_line(size = 0.75) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 29.7, color = "orange1", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(18, 32, by = 7), limits = c(17.5, 33)) + 
  # geom_hline(yintercept = 29.7, color = "black", size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Offshore" = "darkblue", 
                                "Inshore" = "lightblue")) +
  scale_x_datetime(date_labels = "%b %Y", expand = c(0, 0)) +
 
  labs(y = "Temperature (°C)", x = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 9),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
        legend.direction = "horizontal",
        legend.position = c(0.16,0.1))


# Save the temperature plot
ggsave("Plots/Fig_1/temps_plot.png", plot = temps, width = 7.75, height = 1.5, dpi = 300)
```

# Combine Maps and Save Fig 1
```{r Fig 1}
background <- ggplot() +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA))

Fig_1 <- ggdraw(background) +
  draw_plot(map, x = 0, y = .21, width = 0.95, height = 0.8) +
  draw_plot(na_map, x = 0.1, y = .65, width = 0.35, height = 0.35) +
  draw_plot(temps, x = 0.036, y = -0.02, width = 0.915, height = .28) 

ggsave(
  filename = "Plots/Fig_1/Fig_1.pdf",  # Name of the output file
  plot = Fig_1,               # Specify the plot to save
  width = 6,                       # Width in inches
  height = 5                        # Height in inches
)


ggsave(
  filename = "Plots/Fig_1/Fig_1.png",  # Name of the output file
  plot = Fig_1,               # Specify the plot to save
  width = 6,                       # Width in inches
  height = 5                        # Height in inches
)

```
